observations.what_customers_say: Customers value consultation that is understandable and helps them make sense of the offer.
observations.decision_relevance: If the customer does not understand the quote, they are unlikely to move confidently to signature.

observations.what_customers_say: Positive review language connects trust to advice that feels specific to the property.
observations.decision_relevance: A site-specific quote explanation can be more credible than generic solar benefits.

observations.what_customers_say: Customers can delay when multiple offers are structured differently and are hard to compare.
observations.decision_relevance: Comparison support may be more valuable than another persuasion message.

observations.what_customers_say: Review language connects good advice with value, not only the lowest absolute price.
observations.decision_relevance: A value-conscious buyer may need a scope and quality explanation, not just a discount.

observations.what_customers_say: Customers respond to advice that links the technical setup to concrete household benefit.
observations.decision_relevance: The buyer needs to understand why the proposed system fits their goals.

cross_source_notes: Offer comparison and price/value tension also appear in Otovo and Sunvitec review patterns.
On-site specificity is relevant for roof, shading, and consumption concerns.
caveats: Use for qualitative comparison and consultation patterns only.
